title: Matrices: Storing Data Grids
concept: A matrix is a grid of numbers where each row represents a single example and each column represents a specific feature.

exercises.prompt: Recap: how do we represent a single item, like a shoe with size 10 and price $80, as a vector?
exercises.explain: Right — vectors group related numbers. Now, how do we store hundreds of these vectors together?

cards.title: The spreadsheet grid
cards.body: When you look at a spreadsheet of housing data, you see a grid: rows of houses and columns of features (like size and bedrooms). In math, this grid is called a matrix.

cards.title: What is a matrix?
cards.body: A matrix is a rectangular grid of numbers arranged in rows and columns. In machine learning, it is the standard way to store a whole dataset.

cards.title: An apartment building
cards.body: If a vector is a single apartment (a list of rooms), a matrix is the entire apartment building (a grid of apartments stacked on top of each other).

cards.title: A dataset of cars
cards.body: A matrix stores three cars with features `[mpg, weight]`. The rows are the cars, and the columns are mpg and weight: row 1 is [30, 2000], row 2 is [24, 3000], and row 3 is [18, 4000].

cards.title: Not just a 1D list
cards.body: A vector only has one dimension (a single list). A matrix has two dimensions (rows and columns). You can think of it as a vector of vectors!

right.text:
A grid representing multiple items
e.g. 3 houses, 2 features

exercises.prompt: What is a matrix in machine learning?

options.text: A grid of numbers where rows are examples and columns are features

exercises.explain: A matrix is a two-dimensional grid of numbers representing a whole dataset.

exercises.prompt: True or false: in a standard data matrix, each row represents a single feature and each column represents an example.
exercises.explain: False — rows represent examples (like individual houses), while columns represent features (like size or rooms).

exercises.sentence: If a vector represents one house, a ___ represents a list of houses stored in rows and columns.
exercises.explain: A matrix stacks multiple example vectors into a single grid.

exercises.prompt: A matrix of size 3x2 has how many examples (rows) and features (columns)?
exercises.explain: The size is read as rows by columns, so 3x2 means 3 rows (examples) and 2 columns (features).

exercises.prompt: You have a dataset of 100 students, and you measure 4 features for each (grades, attendance, study hours, age). How is this dataset represented as a matrix?

options.text: A grid of 100 rows and 4 columns
A grid of 4 rows and 100 columns

exercises.explain: Excellent! 100 rows (examples) and 4 columns (features) is the standard matrix layout for ML datasets. Next: how to combine features and weights to make predictions!
